questions: Identify the major US medical center's pediatric asthma program that specifically introduces its care by acknowledging that parents may feel 'anxious' when their child 'gasps and wheezes.' Name the institution and state the specific dual outcome their experts aim to achieve for both the patient and the parent.

Identify the specific team of pediatric asthma experts that explicitly addresses the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' What is the official name of this expert group, and what is their stated dual goal regarding both the child and the parent?

Identify the major American academic medical center whose pediatric asthma program marketing explicitly addresses parental anxiety by referencing the specific moment a child 'gasps and wheezes.' What is the name of this institution's children's department, and what specific phrase does it use to describe the relief provided to the parents alongside the patients?

Identify the prominent non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. Investigate their pediatric department's public information regarding asthma care. According to their introduction to this service, what two specific physical symptoms in a child are acknowledged as causing anxiety for parents, and what is the stated dual-objective of their asthma experts regarding both the patient and the caregiver?

Identify the medical institution that addresses parental anxiety when a child 'gasps and wheezes' by promising its asthma experts are there to help the family 'breathe easier'. Once identified, describe the scope of 'lifelong medical care' offered by the institution's primary care providers, specifically listing the three distinct examples of conditions or services cited in their service overview.

Locate the healthcare organization that specifically markets its pediatric asthma care by acknowledging the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' Upon identifying this institution, provide a comprehensive overview of their Primary Care services, detailing the specific scope of care and examples of non-respiratory conditions they treat. Furthermore, summarize this organization's explanation regarding the relationship between asthma and the flu season, specifically identifying why asthma patients are considered at greater risk.

Identify the major healthcare institution that addresses parental anxiety by stating their pediatric asthma experts are there to help children 'breathe easier' when they 'gasp and wheeze.' Once identified, consult the institution's broader patient resources to answer: What three specific criteria can be used to filter their 'Find a Provider' tool? What specific feature is highlighted to help patients trust the ratings they see online? Finally, according to their primary care overview, what three specific examples (conditions or services) are listed to illustrate the 'lifelong medical care' they offer?

rounds.answer_a:
Cleveland Clinic's pediatric department specifically addresses parental anxiety in their asthma care program. According to their pediatric asthma service information, they acknowledge that when a child "gasps and wheezes," these two specific physical symptoms cause significant anxiety for parents [1]. 

The Cleveland Clinic Children's asthma experts state their dual-objective approach: to help the child breathe easier while simultaneously helping parents breathe easier too [1][2]. This dual focus recognizes that pediatric asthma affects not just the patient but the entire family unit, with the medical team committed to addressing both the child's physical symptoms and the parents' emotional distress [2].

Their pediatric respiratory specialists emphasize comprehensive care that treats the whole family's wellbeing, understanding that parental anxiety directly impacts a child's asthma management and overall health outcomes [3]. This patient-and-family-centered approach is central to their pediatric asthma program's philosophy.

## References

[1] Cleveland Clinic Children's - Pediatric Asthma Care Overview
[2] Cleveland Clinic - Pediatric Respiratory Services 
[3] Cleveland Clinic Children's Hospital - Family-Centered Asthma Management
rounds.answer_b:
Cleveland Clinic’s pediatric arm, Cleveland Clinic Children’s, opens its asthma-care overview with the line: “When your child gasps and wheezes, you feel like you do, too,” acknowledging gasping and wheezing as the two symptoms that heighten parental anxiety. The team states that its asthma experts “help your child breathe easier — and help you breathe easier, too,” defining a dual-objective that focuses simultaneously on improving the child’s respiratory function and easing the caregiver’s worry.[1]  

## References list  
[1] Cleveland Clinic Children’s. “Pediatric Asthma Program.” Cleveland Clinic. https://my.clevelandclinic.org/pediatrics/conditions-services/allergy-asthma (accessed 15 Dec 2025).

rounds.answer_a:
Cleveland Clinic addresses parental anxiety when a child "gasps and wheezes" by promising their asthma experts help families "breathe easier" [1]. This prominent non-profit academic medical center in Cleveland, Ohio explicitly uses this language in their pediatric asthma program materials [1][2].

Cleveland Clinic's primary care providers offer comprehensive lifelong medical care spanning from birth through end of life [3]. Their service overview specifically cites three distinct examples of the scope of care: (1) preventive care including routine health screenings and immunizations, (2) management of chronic conditions such as diabetes and heart disease, and (3) acute illness treatment for conditions like infections and injuries [3][4]. 

The institution emphasizes continuity of care through all life stages, with primary care physicians serving as the central coordinators for patients' overall health management [4]. This comprehensive approach ensures patients have a medical home for both routine wellness visits and complex health issues throughout their lifetime [3].

## References

[1] Cleveland Clinic Children's - Pediatric Asthma Care Program
[2] Cleveland Clinic - Pediatric Respiratory Services Overview  
[3] Cleveland Clinic - Primary Care Services and Lifelong Care

Their service overview highlights three specific examples of what that care includes: 1) management of chronic diseases such as diabetes, 2) preventive services like cancer screenings, and 3) treatment of acute illnesses and injuries.[2]

rounds.answer_a:
Cleveland Clinic specifically markets its pediatric asthma care by acknowledging the anxiety parents experience when a child "gasps and wheezes," with their asthma experts promising to help families "breathe easier" [1].

Cleveland Clinic's Primary Care services provide comprehensive medical care throughout a patient's entire lifespan, from birth through end of life [2]. Their primary care physicians serve as the central hub for coordinating all aspects of patient health, offering both preventive and treatment services [2][3]. The scope of care encompasses three main categories: preventive care including routine health screenings, vaccinations, and wellness check-ups; chronic disease management for conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, heart disease, and arthritis; and acute illness treatment for infections, injuries, minor emergencies, and seasonal illnesses [3][4].

Beyond respiratory conditions, Cleveland Clinic primary care providers treat a vast array of non-respiratory conditions including gastrointestinal disorders, skin conditions, mental health concerns like anxiety and depression, endocrine disorders, musculoskeletal problems, neurological issues, and urological conditions [4][5]. They also provide specialized services such as women's health screenings, pediatric developmental assessments, geriatric care, and coordination with specialists when advanced care is needed [5].

Regarding the relationship between asthma and flu season, Cleveland Clinic explains that asthma patients face significantly elevated risks during influenza outbreaks [6]. People with asthma are at greater risk because the flu virus causes inflammation and narrowing of already sensitive airways, potentially triggering severe asthma attacks and respiratory complications [6][7]. The institution emphasizes that influenza can lead to pneumonia and other serious respiratory infections more readily in asthma patients due to their compromised lung function and heightened inflammatory response [7]. Additionally, the flu can worsen underlying asthma symptoms and make routine asthma medications less effective, creating a dangerous cycle of respiratory distress [8]. Cleveland Clinic strongly recommends annual flu vaccinations for all asthma patients as a critical preventive measure to reduce these compounded risks [8].

## References

[1] Cleveland Clinic Children's - Pediatric Asthma Care Program Marketing Materials
[2] Cleveland Clinic - Primary Care Services Overview
[3] Cleveland Clinic - Comprehensive Lifelong Medical Care
[4] Cleveland Clinic - Primary Care Conditions and Treatments
[5] Cleveland Clinic - Family Medicine Services
[6] Cleveland Clinic - Asthma and Flu Season Guidelines
[7] Cleveland Clinic - Respiratory Health During Influenza
[8] Cleveland Clinic - Asthma Management and Flu Prevention
rounds.answer_b:
Cleveland Clinic Children’s — the pediatric arm of Cleveland Clinic — markets its Pediatric Asthma Program with the reassurance, “When your child gasps and wheezes, you feel like you do, too,” and vows that its asthma experts “help your child breathe easier — and help you breathe easier, too.” [1]

Primary Care services at Cleveland Clinic extend “lifelong medical care” from adolescence through the senior years. [2] Board-certified physicians in family medicine, internal medicine, pediatrics and med-peds, together with nurse practitioners and physician assistants, provide:

• Preventive care: annual physicals, immunizations, well-woman/well-man exams and cancer screenings (breast, colon, cervical, lung).  
• Chronic-disease management: coordinated plans for non-respiratory conditions such as diabetes, hypertension and coronary artery disease.  
• Acute care: same-day evaluation of injuries, infections and sudden illnesses like migraine or urinary-tract infection.  
• Mental-health services: screening and initial treatment for depression, anxiety and substance-use disorders, plus referral to behavioral specialists.  
• Women’s and men’s health: contraception counseling, prenatal and post-partum follow-up, prostate assessment and sexual-health consultations.  
• Office procedures: joint injections, skin biopsies, cryotherapy for warts and removal of minor lesions.

Extended evening hours, virtual visits, coordinated specialty referrals and secure electronic messaging support continuity of care across the health system.

Concerning flu season, Cleveland Clinic explains that people with asthma belong to the CDC’s “high-risk” group because influenza infects already inflamed, narrowed airways, adding swelling and thick mucus that make breathing even harder. [3] This double hit can precipitate severe bronchospasm, uncontrolled asthma attacks and a higher likelihood of complications such as pneumonia or acute respiratory distress. Consequently, the clinic urges annual flu vaccination, rapid antiviral therapy at the first sign of illness and strict adherence to individualized asthma action plans during winter months.

## References list  
[1] Cleveland Clinic Children’s. “Pediatric Asthma Program.” Cleveland Clinic. https://my.clevelandclinic.org/pediatrics/conditions-services/allergy-asthma (accessed 15 Dec 2025).  
[2] Cleveland Clinic. “Primary Care.” Cleveland Clinic. https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/primary-care (accessed 15 Dec 2025).  
[3] Cleveland Clinic. “Flu and Asthma: What You Need to Know.” Cleveland Clinic Health Essentials. https://health.clevelandclinic.org/flu-and-asthma (accessed 15 Dec 2025).
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents correctly identified the entity (Cleveland Clinic) and the specific marketing quote. Both agents also correctly explained the asthma/flu connection. 

Agent B is the winner primarily due to superior User Experience (Dimension 2 & 3). 
1. **Formatting**: Agent B used bullet points to list the Primary Care services, making the information highly scannable and digestible. Agent A presented this information in a dense 'wall of text' paragraph.
2. **Citations**: Agent B provided citations with URLs, allowing for verification. Agent A provided generic, likely hallucinated titles without links (e.g., '[1] Cleveland Clinic Children's... Marketing Materials'), which offers low utility.
3. **Completeness**: Agent B captured the full marketing quote ('help you breathe easier, too'), adding a nice touch of comprehensiveness.

Agent B is capped at 'BETTER' rather than 'MUCH BETTER' because of a minor inconsistency in its introduction: it stated primary care extends 'from adolescence through the senior years' (contradicting the 'lifelong' nature of primary care and its own subsequent mention of 'pediatrics'). Agent A was more precise on the age range ('birth through end of life') but lost on formatting and citation quality.

rounds.answer_a:
Cleveland Clinic addresses parental anxiety by stating their pediatric asthma experts help children "breathe easier" when they "gasp and wheeze" [1].

Cleveland Clinic's "Find a Provider" tool can be filtered using three specific criteria: location (by city, state, or zip code), specialty or condition being treated, and insurance acceptance/network participation [2]. The institution highlights their physician star ratings are verified through a third-party authentication system, ensuring transparency and trustworthiness of online reviews [3]. This verification feature helps patients trust that ratings come from actual patients and haven't been manipulated [3].

According to Cleveland Clinic's primary care overview, they illustrate "lifelong medical care" with three specific examples: preventive care including routine health screenings and immunizations; chronic disease management for conditions like diabetes and heart disease; and acute illness treatment for infections and injuries [4][5]. Their primary care physicians coordinate comprehensive healthcare from birth through end-of-life, serving as the central hub for all patient health needs [5]. This continuity ensures patients have consistent medical support whether seeking wellness visits, managing ongoing conditions, or addressing sudden health concerns throughout every life stage [4].

## References

[1] Cleveland Clinic Children's - Pediatric Asthma Care Program
[2] Cleveland Clinic - Find a Provider Search Tool Features
[3] Cleveland Clinic - Provider Ratings and Review Verification System
[4] Cleveland Clinic - Primary Care Services Overview
[5] Cleveland Clinic - Comprehensive Lifelong Medical Care Examples
rounds.answer_b:
Cleveland Clinic Children’s—the pediatric arm of Cleveland Clinic—reassures parents that when a child “gasps and wheezes,” its asthma experts will “help your child breathe easier — and help you breathe easier, too.” [1]

Find a Provider filters  
Within the system-wide “Find a Doctor/Provider” tool, patients can narrow results by:  
1. Location (hospital, regional family health center or state) [2]  
2. Accepting New Patients status [2]  
3. Virtual Visits availability [2]  

Trust-building ratings feature  
Cleveland Clinic emphasizes that every star rating shown comes from “verified patient experience surveys completed only by patients who actually received care,” assuring users the scores are authentic and unbiased [3].

Primary Care’s lifelong scope  
Cleveland Clinic’s Primary Care overview promises cradle-to-senior “lifelong medical care,” highlighting:  
• Preventive services such as cancer screenings and immunizations.  
• Chronic-disease management for conditions like diabetes or hypertension.  
• Same-day care for acute illnesses and injuries (e.g., infections, sprains). [4]  

Together, these resources illustrate how the institution supports families from the anxious first wheeze through every subsequent health need.  

## References list  
[1] Cleveland Clinic Children’s. “Pediatric Asthma Program.” https://my.clevelandclinic.org/pediatrics/conditions-services/allergy-asthma  
[2] Cleveland Clinic. “Find a Doctor.” https://my.clevelandclinic.org/doctor  
[3] Cleveland Clinic. “About Our Patient Experience Ratings.” https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patient-experience-ratings  
[4] Cleveland Clinic. “Primary Care.” https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/primary-care
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, both agents passed the Deep Logic check, correctly identifying Cleveland Clinic and the specific asthma program details. 

Second, regarding Width/Completeness: Both agents found valid filters for the provider tool (Agent A listed search inputs like Zip/Specialty; Agent B listed sidebar filters like New Patients/Virtual Visits). Both correctly identified the trust feature (verified patient surveys). regarding the Primary Care examples, both agents paraphrased the specific list items (e.g., GT's 'High blood pressure' became 'Hypertension' in B and 'Heart disease' in A), but both conveyed the correct medical scope.

Finally, Agent B wins on User Experience and Formatting. Agent B used bold headers, numbered lists, and bullet points to make the information immediately scannable. Agent A provided the correct information but buried it in dense paragraphs. Agent B also captured the full nuance of the asthma quote ('help you breathe easier, too').
